Name Popularity Checker

Enter any name to instantly see its global popularity rank, estimated number of living bearers, and decade-by-decade trend from 1880 to today.

Wondering how many people have my name? The search bar below focuses on trends, but our homepage shows global estimates. Need to compare two names? Try the Name Comparison tool instead. Want to chart up to 4 names over time? Use the Trend Visualizer.

Try a preset example

What is a Name Popularity Checker?

A name popularity checker is a free online tool that uses official birth registry data to show how common a given first name is. It returns three core attributes: a global rank, an estimated number of living people who share the name, and a historical popularity score for each decade since 1880. The HowManyOfMe checker covers 100,000+ names across 80+ countries and updates annually with the newest SSA and ONS data. Want to find out how common your name is? Just use the search bar above.

Key Features and Attributes

Global Popularity Rank

Numeric rank position out of all recorded first names worldwide, recalculated each year.

Estimated Living Bearers

Actuarial life-table adjusted count of how many people with that name are alive today.

Decade Trend Chart

1880s through 2020s popularity score (0–100) showing rises, declines and revivals.

Gender Distribution

Percentage split between male, female and unisex usage for the name.

Country Breakdown

Per-country rank for 80+ nations, including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and India.

Zero Signup

No account, no email, no paywall. Instant results, unlimited lookups.

Who Uses This Tool?

Expecting parents

Scenario: Considering 'Aurora' for a baby girl but worried it's overused.

Result: See Aurora ranks #44 in 2024 and is up 600% since 2010 — popular but not yet saturated.

Authors and screenwriters

Scenario: Need an authentic-feeling 1950s American name for a novel character.

Result: Filter the decade chart to 1950s, find names like 'Linda' or 'Gary' that peaked then.

Genealogists

Scenario: Researching how common 'Mildred' was when their great-grandmother was born.

Result: Decade chart shows Mildred peaked at rank #6 in 1910–1919, confirming it was a top-tier name.

Curious individuals

Scenario: Wondering if their unusual name is truly rare or just feels uncommon locally.

Result: Get an exact bearer estimate and rank — sometimes a 'rare' name actually ranks in the top 200.

Worked Examples

Input: "Emma"

Finding: Rank #1 in the US 2014–2018, ~1.9M living bearers worldwide.

Insight: Classic rising-then-plateauing trend; still safe but no longer ascendant.

Input: "Atlas"

Finding: Rank ~#150 in 2024, up from #800 in 2010.

Insight: A clear breakout name — early-adopter parents are finding it before saturation.

Input: "Bertha"

Finding: Rank #4 in 1880, today below #5,000.

Insight: Textbook decline pattern; ripe for vintage revival in the next decade.

✓ Pros

  • ✓ Free forever, no signup or email required
  • ✓ Covers 100,000+ names across 80+ countries
  • ✓ Annual data refresh from official birth registries
  • ✓ Instant results in under 1 second
  • ✓ Mobile-friendly with shareable result links

✗ Cons

  • ✗ Estimates for very rare names (<100 bearers) carry higher uncertainty
  • ✗ Country breakdown is sparse for nations without public birth registries
  • ✗ Does not yet support full-name (first + last) frequency lookups

80+ country coverage✓ YesUS onlyMostly US/UKUS only
No signup✓ YesAccount neededSome features paywalled✓ Yes
Visual decade chart✓ Built-in✓✓✗ CSV only

Why Name Popularity Matters

Popularity data helps parents balance familiarity with individuality. A name ranked in the top 10 means roughly 1 in 100 children in a classroom may share it. A name ranked outside the top 1,000 is virtually unique in most schools.

For non-parents, popularity context explains why a name "feels" old or trendy — perception is driven by which decade the name peaked in.

Benefits of Checking Name Popularity

  • 📊 Identify whether a name is rare or common
  • 📈 Spot rising trends before they peak
  • 📉 Avoid names rapidly losing popularity
  • ⏳ Find timeless names with stable usage
  • 🌍 Understand regional and cultural distribution

Where the Data Comes From

Popularity calculations combine four authoritative datasets: the U.S. Social Security Administration national birth data (1880–present), the UK Office for National Statistics, Statistics Canada baby name registers, and aggregated census data from 78 additional countries. Living-bearer estimates apply CDC actuarial life tables to historical birth counts.

Read the full methodology page for the exact formulas and sources.

Frequently Asked Questions

How accurate is the Name Popularity Checker?▾

Accuracy is high for English-speaking countries because we combine U.S. SSA birth data (1880–present), UK ONS records, and census tables from 80+ countries. For a name with more than 1,000 bearers, the estimate is typically within ±5% of the true count.

Can I check any name with this tool?▾

Yes — the checker covers more than 100,000 unique first names across English, Spanish, Arabic, Indian, Chinese and African naming traditions. Even rare names return a 'likely fewer than X bearers' estimate.

How is name popularity calculated?▾

Popularity is calculated as a per-decade rank score from 0–100 based on how often the name appeared in birth records during that decade, normalized against the total births in the same period.

Why do some names suddenly become popular?▾

Pop culture is the biggest driver. A hit film (Arya, Khaleesi), a celebrity baby (North, Stormi), a viral TikTok, or a beloved book character can push a name up hundreds of ranks in a single year.

Can name popularity trends predict the future?▾

Trends are not guarantees, but a name with 3+ consecutive decades of growth has an 80% probability of remaining in the top 500 for at least one more decade, based on historical patterns.

What is considered a 'rare' name?▾

A rare name is one given to fewer than 1 in 10,000 babies in a given year. By that definition, roughly 70% of all recorded baby names are rare.

Is the Name Popularity Checker free?▾

Yes. The tool is 100% free, requires no signup, and has no usage limits. You can check unlimited names.

Does the tool show popularity by country?▾

Yes. After running a check, scroll to the country breakdown section to see how the name ranks in the U.S., UK, Canada, Australia, India, and 75+ other countries.

How is this different from baby name books?▾

Books are static and outdated within months. This tool refreshes its dataset annually with the latest birth registry data, so trends always reflect the current year.

Can I use the data for academic research?▾

Yes — the underlying datasets are public-domain (SSA, ONS, census). Cite HowManyOfMe.co as the visualization source if you publish.

Scroll up, type any name, and see where it ranks worldwide in under a second.
